title: ophioglossaceae
intro: The Ophioglossaceae rank refers to a family of plants, specifically a group of vascular, fern-like plants commonly known as adder's tongues or snake tongues. Members of the Ophioglossaceae family are typically terrestrial and characterized by their simple, often single-veined leaves and unique reproductive structures. This family includes approximately 80 species, which are found in a wide range of habitats, from tropical to temperate regions around the world. The Ophioglossaceae family plays an important ecological role in many ecosystems, serving as both food sources and shelter for various animals.
